refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and the operational context ensures clarity and testability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the agreed evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Maintain network infrastructure reliability to support link availability.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network paths and failover mechanisms.
DP2: Use real-time link health monitoring and automatic reconnection protocols.
DP3: Employ robust error detection and correction algorithms on the link.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network paths and failover mechanisms.

potential_sources: Link monitoring tightly coupled with network infrastructure management.
Failover mechanisms require coordination between teleoperation and network modules.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates link availability concerns and supports modular implementation.

assumptions_risks: Risk of increased complexity leading to potential integration issues.
Assumes failover mechanisms do not introduce unacceptable latency.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating error correction algorithms if failover alone does not meet availability targets.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for teleoperation link management, including monitoring, failover, and recovery.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to the physical and logical network infrastructure enabling teleoperation communication.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides link status and health data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands dependent on link availability.

module_containment_scores.notes: Contains overall system logic but link availability is diffused across modules.
Vehicle module depends on link but does not control availability mechanisms.
Primary containment of link availability logic and mechanisms.
Receives alerts but does not manage link availability.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability management within Teleoperation minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential single point of failure if Teleoperation module is compromised.
Integration complexity with network infrastructure may require cross-module coordination.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate adding fallback monitoring in NGHE Solution to mitigate single point of failure.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear mapping and testable verification.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion of link status data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.
Risk of interface mismatches if network link protocols evolve.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module effectively contains the DP with minimal diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring link availability metrics via Teleoperation interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of link availability within Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Consider fallback monitoring in NGHE Solution to reduce single point of failure risk.
Define interface versioning strategy for Network Link to handle protocol changes.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, risks related to single point of failure and interface evolution warrant refinement before proceeding.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Maintain network infrastructure reliability to support link availability.

revise.why: To incorporate fallback monitoring and improve interface robustness.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Teleoperation module can implement required monitoring and recovery.
open_risks: Single point of failure in Teleoperation module.
Interface protocol changes impacting Network Link.
evidence_needed: Test data on failover effectiveness and latency.
